traces.mapping_rationale: The notebook treats the store lookup as a prerequisite for any credible Rossmann baseline because the daily train/test tables are missing the static store metadata by themselves.
The notebook explicitly inspects `Date` as a forecasting anchor, so parsing it stays in the final baseline.
Year, month, and day are the minimal calendar parts surfaced in the notebook and belong in a believable first-pass forecasting pipeline.
After freezing the plain-language shortlist, I kept exactly one closed-store rule and mapped it to the simpler `Open == 1` branch.

traces.section: Closure Handling, Leakage, And Sparse Metadata
traces.mapping_rationale: The notebook highlights `CompetitionDistance` as one of the clearest store-metadata gaps, which supports keeping a basic median repair in the manual baseline.
The shortlist called for one simple encoding strategy over the low-cardinality categorical block; bank alignment mapped that choice to the label-encoding representative.

traces.section: Closure Handling, Leakage, And Sparse Metadata
traces.mapping_rationale: The notebook explicitly checks that `Customers` is train-only, so dropping it remains one of the clearest defensible manual decisions.
